regions: Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA)

dependencies: Single-source or sole-source suppliers with no alternatives identified
Reliance on helium, iodine, and rare earth minerals
Dependence on third-party transport and warehouse management services
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical conflicts (Russia-Ukraine, Middle East tensions)
Concentration risk in China for manufacturing and sourcing
risks: Supply chain interruptions from war, geopolitical conflicts, government sanctions, trade controls, tariffs
Natural disasters, climate change-related physical and transitional risks
Public health crises, epidemics, pandemics (COVID-19 impact)
Cybersecurity incidents or disruptions impacting IT systems
Supplier failures to meet delivery obligations, price increases, or cessation of supply
Shortages or delays in raw materials (helium, iodine, rare earth minerals)
Capacity constraints, production disruptions, price fluctuations
Localization requirements and government policies favoring local suppliers (China, Russia)
Increased costs from tariffs, duties, currency fluctuations, taxes
Third-party supplier quality failures or non-compliance with regulations

regulatory_risks: FDA and equivalent global agencies tightly regulate medical devices and pharmaceutical products

Rigorous regulation governing development, product testing, manufacturing, packaging, labeling, safety, quality, storage, marketing clearance/approval, advertising, promotion, import/export, sales, distribution, performance, effectiveness, post-market surveillance
Regulatory premarket clearance, approval, or conformity assessment requirements may delay ability to market new offerings
Periodic inspections by regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, NMPA in China)
Adverse inspection outcomes may impact ability to develop, manufacture, market, or distribute products
Product recalls, removals, replacement, servicing, or corrective actions
Regulatory scrutiny may increase in the future
Regulations increasing and becoming more stringent in already regulated countries
Countries that did not previously regulate medical products are developing and implementing regulations
Cloud, edge computing, clinical decision support software, cybersecurity, mobile medical applications, AI, generative AI, Machine Learning subject to rapidly evolving and changing regulations

Regulatory authorities may refuse to accept or authorize regulatory filings, ban products, detain or seize products, order recalls, impose operating restrictions, enjoin violations, assess civil or criminal penalties
FCPA and similar anti-corruption and anti-bribery laws globally (UKBA, China Anti-Unfair Competition Law)
Anti-kickback and false claims laws (U.S. AKS, FCA)
Antitrust and competition laws
Stringent privacy laws and information security regulations (HIPAA, GDPR, U.K. GDPR, Brazil LGPD, China PIPL, China DSL, India PDPL, California Consumer Privacy Act)
Data localization laws mandating certain data be stored within specific jurisdictions
Laws and regulations governing government contracts, public procurement, government reimbursements
Federal Acquisition Regulation, Procurement Integrity Act, Buy American Act, Trade Agreements Act (U.S.)
Sunshine Act (U.S. federal Physician Payments Sunshine Act)
Defense Production Act (DPA) may require prioritization of U.S. federal government contracts
Environmental, health, and safety (EH&S) laws and regulations
Climate change mitigation and adaptation regulation, tax, disclosure, and reporting requirements
Radioactive materials handling and disposal regulations
Tax laws and regulations (GILTI, OECD Pillar Two GloBE rules, state tax laws)
Right-to-repair laws in the United States and elsewhere
EU data legislation laws
New or changing regulations on AI development and use (EU AI regulations)

sanction_risks: Russia and Ukraine conflict: sanctions imposed by U.S., EU, and other countries affecting ability to supply customers in Russia
U.S. Department of Commerce regulations requiring licenses for exports to Russia (License Exception for medical equipment)
EU and other countries expanded licensing requirements for spare parts, services, software to Russia
U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) administers laws restricting transactions with sanctioned countries/entities
U.S. Department of Commerce Bureau of Industry and Security administers export controls
Sanctions and restrictions on Cuba, Iran, Syria, North Korea
Potential inability to repatriate earnings from Russia and Ukraine
Risk of exclusion from government healthcare programs (Medicare, Medicaid) for FCPA violations
Suspension or debarment from government contract work for non-compliance
China anti-corruption campaign impacting healthcare sector, delayed orders and sales
Geopolitical tensions (Middle East, Russia-Ukraine) may heighten cyber-attack risks
Trade protectionism, tariffs, non-tariff barriers, export controls (U.S., China, EU)
Fragmentation of global economy, reduction in international trade
Economic nationalism making it difficult to attract/retain customers or maintain sales
